subject: Re: unbinned k3 tomograms on RTX3090
body:
Hi Dan,

Do you mean you reached Nyquist with M, and wanted to process further with 
some of the superres frequencies included? I wouldn't go to completely 
unbinned (unless you have reason to believe you'll bump into that Nyquist 
as well), but rather something fractional, e.g. 0.8x. This way the memory 
requirements won't completely explode.

You also only need to reprocess the raw movies in Warp with that new 
binning setting to generate averages. After that, you can upscale the 
half-maps from M using e.g. Relion to get to that smaller pixel size, and 
create a new species with them. No need to reprocess the tilt series or 
redo the template matching in Warp. Your next refinement iteration in M 
should produce maps with superres data included.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Friday, March 26, 2021 at 4:14:36 PM UTC+1 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Dan,
>
> Glad you got a bit further, keep fighting the good fight!! Nothing 
> immediately comes to mind, can you reconstruct at a smaller pixel size from 
> the same data? (Tilt series in warp still unbinned, reconstructing 
> particles at a significantly larger pixel size) This might help to rule out 
> the ‘aliasing free box size’ type issues
>
> Good luck!
>
> Alister
>
> > On 26 Mar 2021, at 15:09, Daniel Mann <d.m...@fz-juelich.de> wrote:
> > 
> > Dear Alister and all,
> > 
> > using only one GPU indeed helped me to cross the barrier for CTF 
> estimation. Reconstruction and template matching worked fine as well, 
> thanks a lot :)
> > 
> > However, subtomo reconstruction with 256px boxes produces completely 
> black subtomos.
> > 
> > From the mail archive:
> > 
> > - I'm using english layout ("." separator instead of ",")
> > 
> > - I don't have phase shift (no phase plate)
> > 
> > - no error message that GPU runs out of memory. Actually tried setting 
> 64px boxes with same px size and 64px boxes with binned pixel size; still 
> black.
> > 
> > Any ideas?
> > 
> > Cheers,
> > 
> > Dan
> > 
> > Also tried skipping normalisation, no success.
> > 
> > Am 11.03.2021 um 09:52 schrieb Alister Burt:
> >> Hi Dan,
> >> 
> >> Your workstation sounds like a beast!!
> >> 
> >> I’m not sure it will help in your case but I had luck using only 1 GPU 
> to halve RAM requirements in M (Dimitry said multiple GPUs share a subset 
> of your RAM).
> >> 
> >> Depending on resolution/number of particles per field of view you might 
> have a really hard time getting this to run in M at all even if CTF 
> estimation works, might be worth stepping down the pixel size a bit less? 
> Nyquist is always exciting!
> >> 
> >> There have also been quite a few problems reported with 3090 cards and 
> Warp which might not be helping your situation (Tom Dendooven and Teige 
> Matthews-Palmer have been on the list about it, maybe others I’m forgetting 
> too)
> >> 
> >> Cheers and good luck!
> >> 
> >> Alister
> >> 
> >> 
> >> 
> >>> On 11 Mar 2021, at 08:29, Daniel Mann <d.m...@fz-juelich.de> wrote:
> >>> 
> >>> ﻿Hi all,
> >>> 
> >>> after bin1 data from K3 tomograms refined to Nyquist in Warp/M I tried
> >>> to repeat the process for unbinned K3 superres data (11k x 8k) on 2x
> >>> RTX3090 GPUs (24GB each, 64 CPUs, 256GB RAM).
> >>> 
> >>> Warp crashes at CTF estimation after IMOD alignment with
> >>> 
> >>> System.AccessViolationException: Attempted to read or write protected
> >>> memory. This is often an indication that other memory is corrupt.
> >>> 
> >>> Which I interpret as an out-of-memory message, although I don't really
> >>> see a strong GPU memory peak in Task Manager.
> >>> 
> >>> If my interpretation is correct, is there any strategy to reduce GPU
> >>> memory usage, e.g. CTF window size, range or other cmd tweaks? I'm not
> >>> using any denoising or so and use 1 progress per GPU in the global 
> settings.
> >>> 
> >>> Thanks for your help!
> >>> 
> >>> Best wishes from Jülich
> >>> 
> >>> Dan
> >>> 
> >>> 
> >>> 
> >>>
